content: Pediatric dosing calculations: Clark's rule (child dose = adult dose × weight(lb)/150) largely replaced by direct mg/kg dosing. Young's rule (child dose = adult dose × age/(age+12)) outdated. Current approach: use evidence-based pediatric dosing references (Harriet Lane, Red Book, Lexicomp Pediatric). Therapeutic drug monitoring crucial for narrow TI drugs: vancomycin, aminoglycosides, anticonvulsants. Growth and development monitoring: assess impact of chronic medications on growth velocity, pubertal development, bone health (steroids), neurodevelopment (stimulants, antiepileptics). Adverse drug reaction recognition: children may present differently than adults (valproate hepatotoxicity more common in young children); nonverbal children require observation for behavioral changes. Medication error prevention: double-check calculations, use metric units only, avoid trailing zeros, specify concentration on prescriptions.
